member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext
member.summary: Holds the active data isolation and permission configuration for the MCP server session. Registered as a singleton; populated from McpServer appsettings on startup.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext.Level
member.summary: Active isolation level. Defaults to MultiTenant.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext.TenantIdentifier
member.summary: Tenant identifier used when Level is Tenant. All tool calls are automatically scoped to this tenant.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext.Permissions
member.summary: Role permission levels for this deployment. Defaults to Full for all roles when absent from configuration.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext.Validate
member.summary: Validates the isolation context at startup. Throws if configuration is incomplete or uses an invalid combination.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationLevel
member.summary: Data isolation level for the MCP server. Controls which tenants, divisions, and employees are accessible within a session.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationLevel.MultiTenant
member.summary: Full access across all tenants. No automatic scoping is applied.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationLevel.Tenant
member.summary: Access restricted to a single tenant configured at startup via McpServer:TenantIdentifier.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationLevel.Division
member.summary: Access restricted to a single division within a tenant. (planned)

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationLevel.Employee
member.summary: Access restricted to a single employee — self-service scenarios. (planned)

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermission
member.summary: Access level granted for a role in this MCP Server deployment. Ordered: None < Read < Full — use >= comparisons for minimum permission checks.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermission.None
member.summary: Role tools are not registered — invisible to the AI agent.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermission.Read
member.summary: Read and query tools only. Write tools are excluded even if available.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermission.Full
member.summary: Read and write tools. Required for mutation operations such as start_tenant.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermissions
member.summary: Permission levels per role for this MCP Server deployment. Populated from McpServer:Permissions in appsettings.json or environment variables. Default when the Permissions section is absent: Full for all roles.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermissions.HR
member.summary: Permission for HR tools (divisions, employees, case values).

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermissions.Payroll
member.summary: Permission for Payroll tools (payrolls, payruns, jobs, temporal queries).

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermissions.Regulation
member.summary: Permission for Regulation tools (regulations, wage types, lookups).

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermissions.System
member.summary: Permission for System tools (tenant setup, user management).

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermissions.GetPermission(PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpRole)
member.summary: Returns the permission granted for the given role.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermissions.IsGranted(PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpRole,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermission)
member.summary: Returns true if the given role is granted at least the required permission.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermissions.FromConfiguration(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration)
member.summary: Reads McpServer:Permissions from configuration. Returns a fully-granted instance when the section is absent.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpRole
member.summary: Functional domain to which a tool class belongs. Each tool class is assigned to exactly one role.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpRole.HR
member.summary: Employee master data and organisational structure: divisions, employees, case values.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpRole.Payroll
member.summary: Payroll execution and result verification: payrolls, payruns, payrun jobs, temporal case value queries.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpRole.Regulation
member.summary: Regulation definitions: regulations, wage types, lookups.

member.name: F:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpRole.System
member.summary: Tenant and user management.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.ToolRoleAttribute
member.summary: Declares the role and minimum permission required to register a tool class. Applied to classes decorated with [McpServerToolType]. Tool classes without this attribute are always registered regardless of permissions.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.ToolRoleAttribute.Role
member.summary: The functional domain this tool class belongs to.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.ToolRoleAttribute.Required
member.summary: Minimum permission level required to register this tool class. Defaults to Read.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.ToolRoleAttribute.#ctor(PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpRole,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermission)
member.param: The role this tool class belongs to.
Minimum permission to register. Default: Read.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PayrollTools.CaseValueTimeTools
member.summary: MCP tools for time-based case value queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PayrollTools.CaseValueTimeTools.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Client.PayrollHttpClient,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext)
member.summary: MCP tools for time-based case value queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PayrollTools.CaseValueTimeTools.GetCaseTimeValuesAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Get case values at a specific point in time

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PayrollTools.PayrollQueryTools
member.summary: MCP tools for payroll, payrun and job queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PayrollTools.PayrollQueryTools.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Client.PayrollHttpClient,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext)
member.summary: MCP tools for payroll, payrun and job queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PayrollTools.PayrollQueryTools.ListPayrollsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: List all payrolls of a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PayrollTools.PayrollQueryTools.GetPayrollAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Get a payroll by name within a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PayrollTools.PayrollQueryTools.ListPayrunsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: List all payruns of a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PayrollTools.PayrollQueryTools.ListPayrunJobsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: List all payrun jobs of a tenant, ordered by creation date descending

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PayrollTools.PayrollQueryTools.ListPayrollWageTypesAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: List all effective wage types of a payroll merged across all regulation layers

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.CaseValueQueryTools
member.summary: MCP tools for case value queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.CaseValueQueryTools.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Client.PayrollHttpClient,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext)
member.summary: MCP tools for case value queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.CaseValueQueryTools.ListEmployeeCaseValuesAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: List all employee case values within a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.CaseValueQueryTools.ListCompanyCaseValuesAsync(System.String)
member.summary: List all company case values of a tenant

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.DivisionQueryTools
member.summary: MCP tools for division queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.DivisionQueryTools.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Client.PayrollHttpClient,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext)
member.summary: MCP tools for division queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.DivisionQueryTools.ListDivisionsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: List all divisions of a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.DivisionQueryTools.GetDivisionAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Get a division by name within a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.DivisionQueryTools.GetDivisionAttributeAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Get a single attribute value of a division

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.EmployeeQueryTools
member.summary: MCP tools for employee queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.EmployeeQueryTools.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Client.PayrollHttpClient,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext)
member.summary: MCP tools for employee queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.EmployeeQueryTools.ListEmployeesAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: List employees of a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.EmployeeQueryTools.GetEmployeeAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Get an employee by identifier within a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.EmployeeQueryTools.GetEmployeeAttributeAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Get a single attribute value of an employee

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.UserQueryTools
member.summary: MCP tools for user queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.UserQueryTools.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Client.PayrollHttpClient,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext)
member.summary: MCP tools for user queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.UserQueryTools.ListUsersAsync(System.String)
member.summary: List all users of a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.UserQueryTools.GetUserAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Get a user by identifier within a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.PeopleTools.UserQueryTools.GetUserAttributeAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Get a single attribute value of a user

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.RegulationTools.RegulationQueryTools
member.summary: MCP tools for regulation queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.RegulationTools.RegulationQueryTools.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Client.PayrollHttpClient,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext)
member.summary: MCP tools for regulation queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.RegulationTools.RegulationQueryTools.ListRegulationsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: List all regulations of a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.RegulationTools.RegulationQueryTools.GetRegulationAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Get a regulation by name within a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.RegulationTools.RegulationQueryTools.ListWageTypesAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: List all wage types of a regulation within a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.RegulationTools.RegulationQueryTools.ListLookupsAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: List all lookups of a regulation within a tenant

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.RegulationTools.RegulationQueryTools.ListLookupValuesAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: List all values of a lookup within a regulation

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.StartTenantResult
member.summary: Result of a StartTenant operation

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.StartTenantResult.TenantIdentifier
member.summary: The tenant identifier

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.StartTenantResult.TenantId
member.summary: The internal tenant id assigned by the backend

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.StartTenantResult.CreatedUsers
member.summary: Identifiers of users created during this operation

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.StartTenantResult.CreatedDivisions
member.summary: Names of divisions created during this operation

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.StartTenantResult.Errors
member.summary: Error messages collected during this operation

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.StartTenantResult.Success
member.summary: True when no errors occurred

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.StartTenantTool
member.summary: MCP tools for tenant setup

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.StartTenantTool.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Client.PayrollHttpClient,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext)
member.summary: MCP tools for tenant setup

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.StartTenantTool.StartTenantAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new tenant with an initial admin user and one or more divisions. Equivalent to the Console StartTenant command, but callable by AI agents. Idempotent: skips creation of objects that already exist.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.TenantQueryTools
member.summary: MCP tools for tenant queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.TenantQueryTools.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Client.PayrollHttpClient,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext)
member.summary: MCP tools for tenant queries

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.TenantQueryTools.ListTenantsAsync
member.summary: List all tenants

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.TenantQueryTools.GetTenantAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Get a tenant by identifier

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.TenantTools.TenantQueryTools.GetTenantAttributeAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Get a single tenant attribute by name

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase
member.summary: Base class for all MCP tool classes. Provides typed service factories, identifier-to-id resolvers, and uniform error handling. AI agents work exclusively with external string keys (Identifier/Name); internal integer IDs are resolved here before calling id-based service methods.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.#ctor(PayrollEngine.Client.PayrollHttpClient,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.IsolationContext)
member.summary: Base class for all MCP tool classes. Provides typed service factories, identifier-to-id resolvers, and uniform error handling. AI agents work exclusively with external string keys (Identifier/Name); internal integer IDs are resolved here before calling id-based service methods.

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.HttpClient
member.summary: The Payroll HTTP client used for backend communication

member.name: P:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.Isolation
member.summary: The active data isolation context. Every tool call is filtered through this context.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.ActiveQuery(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String)
member.summary: Builds a Query with status=Active filter, optionally combined with an additional OData filter. All list operations use this to exclude inactive objects by default.
member.param: Optional additional OData filter from the caller

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.ActiveDivisionQuery(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Builds a DivisionQuery with status=Active filter. Required for EmployeeService.QueryAsync which expects DivisionQuery.
member.param: Optional additional OData filter from the caller

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.IsolatedTenantQuery
member.summary: Builds a tenant-scoped active query. In Tenant isolation mode, adds an identifier filter so list_tenants only returns the configured tenant.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.Error(System.Exception)
member.summary: Serializes a caught exception as a structured JSON error result. All public tool methods must catch exceptions and delegate to this method so the MCP client always receives valid JSON, never a raw exception message.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.TenantService
member.summary: Creates a new tenant service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.UserService
member.summary: Creates a new user service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.DivisionService
member.summary: Creates a new division service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.EmployeeService
member.summary: Creates a new employee service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.RegulationService
member.summary: Creates a new regulation service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.WageTypeService
member.summary: Creates a new wage type service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.LookupService
member.summary: Creates a new lookup service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.LookupValueService
member.summary: Creates a new lookup value service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.PayrollService
member.summary: Creates a new payroll service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.PayrunService
member.summary: Creates a new payrun service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.PayrunJobService
member.summary: Creates a new payrun job service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.PayrollResultService
member.summary: Creates a new payroll result service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.EmployeeCaseValueService
member.summary: Creates a new employee case value service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.CompanyCaseValueService
member.summary: Creates a new company case value service

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.EffectiveTenant(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the effective tenant identifier, enforcing isolation. In Tenant mode the configured identifier overrides whatever the AI agent passed. Division and Employee levels are not yet supported and throw NotSupportedException.
member.param: Identifier provided by the AI agent

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.ResolveTenantAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a tenant by its identifier, applying isolation enforcement. In Tenant mode, the configured tenant identifier always takes precedence.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.ResolveTenantContextAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a TenantServiceContext by tenant identifier

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.ResolveUserAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a user by tenant identifier and user identifier

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.ResolveDivisionAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a division by tenant identifier and division name

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.ResolveEmployeeAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves an employee by tenant identifier and employee identifier

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.ResolveEmployeeContextAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves an EmployeeServiceContext by tenant and employee identifier. Required for EmployeeCaseValueService which needs EmployeeServiceContext.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.ResolveRegulationContextAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a RegulationServiceContext by tenant identifier and regulation name

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.ResolveLookupContextAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a LookupServiceContext by tenant, regulation and lookup name. Required for LookupValueService which needs LookupServiceContext.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolBase.ResolvePayrollContextAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a PayrollServiceContext by tenant identifier and payroll name

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolRegistrar
member.summary: Filters and registers MCP tool classes based on the active McpPermissions. Tool classes tagged with [ToolRole] are only registered when the deployment grants at least the required permission for that role. Tool classes without [ToolRole] are always registered.

member.name: M:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolRegistrar.GetPermittedTypes(System.Reflection.Assembly,PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.Isolation.McpPermissions)
member.summary: Returns all tool types from the given assembly that pass the permission filter.

member.name: T:PayrollEngine.McpServer.Tools.ToolsMarker
member.summary: Assembly marker for MCP tool discovery
